body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Nanny Ogg device and cron wiring
body.intention: I intend to verify that introducing Nanny Ogg as the cron device did not cause scheduling logic to leak into Granny or Igor.
body.description:
**What changed:** Nanny Ogg device created as the rack's cron/scheduler; OS cron wired through her fascia.
**Predicted unintended effects:** Scheduling calls that were previously implicit (hardcoded timers in Granny or Igor) may now conflict with Nanny Ogg's ownership; Windows crontab abstraction may be a stub that silently does nothing; fascia slash commands may not reach the OS cron if the web server runs as a different user.
**Signals to watch:** grep devices/granny devices/igor for any scheduling/timer logic added after this ships; test /cron list on a fresh shell to verify user context; check Windows CI if it exists.
**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-23.

**Affected files:** None — observation and verification only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe predicted effects; annotate outcome as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Test plan:** no tests — this is an observation ticket
